ddls:
CREATE TABLE "stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the station */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the station */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the station */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the station */,
  "elevation" TEXT /* Elevation of the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_formats" (
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data format */,
  "format_name" TEXT /* Name of the data format */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data format */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the data format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("format_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "observations" (
  "obs_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observation */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the station where the observation was made */,
  "date" TEXT /* Calendar date and time of observation */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Calendar year of observation */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Calendar month of observation */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Calendar day of observation */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hours - GMT of observation */,
  "data" TEXT /* JSON object containing the actual weather data (e.g., temperature, humidity, wind speed) */,
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data format used for this observation */,
  "quality_control" INTEGER /* Indicates if the observation has passed quality control checks */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("obs_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_format_id FOREIGN KEY ("format_id") REFERENCES data_formats ("format_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "obs_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observation being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the observation */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the observation was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method of access (e.g., API, web interface) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_obs_id FOREIGN KEY ("obs_id") REFERENCES observations ("obs_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each research team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the research team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the research team */,
  "leader_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team leader */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "team_members" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the research team */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id", "user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_versions" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset */,
  "obs_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observation */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the observation in the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id", "obs_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_type" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "observation_types" (
  "obs_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observation */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("obs_id", "type_id")
)
